---
species: "Grateloupia turuturu"
accepted_code: "GRTU"
alternate_codes: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
common_name: "Devil's tongue weed"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Red algae that is morphologically plastic but often looks like a thin red Saccharina." # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/grateloupia_turuturu_TheSeaweedSite.jpg'
image_credit: 'Photo copywright Cornwall, David Fenwick, Snr., from [The Seaweed Site](https://www.seaweed.ie/descriptions/Grateloupia_turuturu.php)' # MUST have an image credit and link to webpage NOT just the image
categories:
  - erect red algae
sampling_programs:
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
    - lsw_microquad
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
  - marsh
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300922
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 295880
---


**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Morphologically plastic, but often looks like red Saccharina. Flat blade that is often slippery and a reddish-brown color. Can exceed 1m in length.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Palmaria palmata* (PAPA) and *Porphyra* spp. (PORS). GRTU has a slimy or slippery texture. PAPA has a more leathery firm texture. POSP grows in very thin, delicate blades that are often translucent and smaller than GRTU.

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=295880)**
